violations.description: Tenant/orgUnit/neighbor defaults are static across tests, which can accumulate state in long-lived environments.
violations.suggestion: Parameterize identifiers with deterministic per-test suffixes for all mutable entities.

violations.description: Tests create mappings/threads but do not perform explicit teardown, relying on idempotent behavior and tolerant assertions.
violations.suggestion: Add cleanup hooks or reset helpers for created thread/mapping artifacts when running against persistent environments.

recommendations: Introduce lightweight teardown helpers for story e.2 synthetic artifacts
Use per-test deterministic context suffixing for static fixture IDs where feasible
Keep idempotent response handling ([200,201]) for resilient setup
summary: Isolation is generally good for CI-style ephemeral runs, with moderate risk in persistent environments due to static fixture IDs and missing explicit teardown.
